prompt: Create a finished editorial paper-collage still for a 9:16 image-to-video B-roll clip. Visual proposition: a low bending branch bearing three large ripe fruits whose skins are collaged from halftone document-paper, form-grid and chat-bubble textures, hanging within easy reach, while a relaxed human hand slips in to pluck the lowest fruit — sensitive personal data turned into low-hanging fruit that is now easy to pick. Scene: perfectly flat mustard-yellow paper field (#B8892A) with subtle uncoated paper fiber. Style: premium editorial stop-motion paper collage; black-and-white halftone photographic cut-outs of a low bending branch, three oversized ripe fruits with document-paper and form-grid textured skins, and a relaxed reaching hand, with selective cream-white and brick-red (#C8451B) colored cardstock accents. Composition: vertical 9:16 locked poster frame; central subject within the middle 70 percent; generous clean color-field negative space; 3 to 6 large separable paper groups suitable for later assemble-from-empty animation. Materials: visible printed halftone dots, crisp machine-cut edges, thin warm-cream paper keylines, soft low-opacity physical drop shadows. Constraint: the relationship must read instantly — document-skinned fruit hanging low, effortlessly picked by hand. Avoid: no typography, no readable letters, no numerals, no logos, no watermark, no UI, no subtitles, no glossy 3D, no photoreal environment, no clutter.
